AI Hallucinates Up to 82% of the Time. Here’s How to Stop Trusting It Blindly.

8 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

photo from aiAI never sounds unsure. Even when it’s making things up.
Nobody warned you about that part.
A few months back I asked a chatbot something about a topic I genuinely knew. Not obscure. Just something I happened to know well.
It came back with a confident, detailed, beautifully answer.
It was wrong.
Not slightly off. Wrong enough that if I’d repeated it to someone, I’d have looked foolish, and I’d have had no idea why.
That’s hallucination.
It’s the industry’s polite way of saying “made this up while sounding completely sure of itself.”
A 2026 study found AI-written summaries were wrong 60% of the time, and people went on to make real purchase decisions based on those wrong summaries. In a separate peer-reviewed medical study, one popular chatbot got things wrong 91.4% of the time.
